Now I need to compute the burndown chart data for the last 10 days (April 24 - May 3, 2026). A burndown chart shows open issues over time. For each day, I need to calculate how many issues were open at the end of that day. An issue is open on a given day if: - It was created on or before that day, AND - It was either not closed, or closed after that day Let me compute this with a Python script, then render it as a bar/line chart. The 10 days are: April 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, May 1, 2, 3. Writing calculation script...

2026-04-11T09:06:18Z 2026-04-11T16:04:11Z 1 CLOSED 2026-04-11T09:06:17Z 2026-04-11T12:56:23Z''' issues = [] for line in raw.strip().split('\n'): parts = line.split('\t') num = int(parts[0]) state = parts[1] created = datetime.fromisoformat(parts[2].replace('Z', '+00:00')) closed = None if len(parts) > 3 and parts[3].strip(): closed = datetime.fromisoformat(parts[3].strip().replace('Z', '+00:00')) issues.append((num, state, created, closed)) # Last 10 days: Apr 24 to May 3 from datetime import timezone days = [] for i in range(10): d = datetime(2026, 4, 24, tzinfo=timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=i) days.append(d) labels = [] open_counts = [] for day in days: end_of_day = day.replace(hour=23, minute=59, second=59) count = 0 for num, state, created, closed in issues: if created <= end_of_day: if closed is None or closed > end_of_day: count += 1 labels.append(day.strftime('%b %d')) open_counts.append(count) print(json.dumps({'labels': labels, 'open_counts': open_counts})) " ", description="Compute open issue counts per day for last 10 days")

Open in dashboard
Download PNG

Here's the burndown chart for the last 10 days, pushed to your dashboard: [Open in dashboard](http://127.0.0.1:8421/public/charts/70e7453b35f5.html) · [Download PNG](http://127.0.0.1:8421/renderpng?src=/public/charts/70e7453b35f5.html) **Summary:** | Period | Open Issues | |--------|------------| | Apr 24–25 | 12 (stable) | | Apr 26–27 | 13→14 (new issues filed) | | Apr 28–29 | 11→10 (big cleanup, 4 issues closed) | | Apr 30–May 3 | 13 (new batch created on Apr 30 & May 3, but several also closed same-day) | The count is currently at **13 open issues**. There was a nice dip on Apr 29 (down to 10) after a productive closure sprint, but new issues on Apr 30 and today brought it back up. Most of today's new issues were opened and closed within minutes — the 4 that remain open are `#152`, `#165`, `#167`, and `#171`.
